# Amazon Bedrock Connector - Full Documentation

# Amazon Bedrock Connector - Additional Configuration Information

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Amazon Bedrock Connector provides detailed configuration information for each operation type. Select
the operation you want to configure.

- [Configure Agent Operations](configuring-agent-operations)
- [Configure Chat Operations](configuring-chat-operations)
- [Configure Embedding Operations](configuring-embedding-operations)
- [Configure Foundational Model Operations](configuring-foundational-model-operations)
- [Configure Image Generation Operations](configuring-image-generation-operations)
- [Configure Sentiment Analysis Operations](configuring-sentiment-analysis-operations)
---

# Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure Amazon Bedrock Connector 1.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

**Open Beta Release**: The **cloud IDE is in open beta**. Any use of Anypoint Code Builder in its
beta state is subject to the applicable beta services terms and conditions, available from the IDE.

To use Anypoint Code Builder to configure a connector or module, create a basic integration project
in Anypoint Code Builder, add connectors and other components to your Mule application to process
your business logic, and configure the attributes.

## Before You Begin

Before creating an integration project, you must:

- [Set up and access the Anypoint Code Builder web or desktop IDE](../../anypoint-code-builder/start-acb)
- Have credentials to access the connectorâs API
- Ensure that the Visual Studio Code Extension Pack for Java is installed.

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)

## Create a New Integration Project

To create a new integration project:

1.  In the activity bar of the IDE, click the
    ![Anypoint Code Builder icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-icon-ms-logo.png) (**Anypoint Code
    Builder**) icon.
2.  From **Quick Actions**, click **Develop an Integration**:

    ![Develop an Integration link highlighted in the Getting started section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/develop-integration-option-mat.png)

3.  Complete the following in the **Develop an Integration** form:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Name</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique name for your project.</p></div><div><p>This name is used as the title and name of the integration project file. For example, if the project name is "Salesforce Integration," the project file name is <code>salesforce-integration</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Location</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your home directory or another directory you create. Click <strong>Browse</strong> to select a different directory for the integration project.</p></div><div><p>Donât create the project within another project directory.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="../../anypoint-code-builder/start-add-folders">Adding Folders to Your Home Directory</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Select the type of project to create by selecting either the **Empty Project** or **Template or
    Example** card.
5.  Select the **Mule runtime** and **Java Version** for your app.
6.  Click **Create Project**.

## Add the Connector to Your Integration Project

Anypoint Connectors provide operations for retrieving, modifying, and sending data to and from
systems.

In addition to the
[built-in connectors](../../anypoint-code-builder/acb-reference#builtin-connectors) that Anypoint
Code Builder provides, you can download many other connectors from Anypoint Exchange.

To import a connector from Exchange and add it to your configuration:

1.  In the Explorer view, open the configuration XML file for your project, such as
    `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Click the
    ![Show Mule graphical mode icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-tree-view.png) (**Show
    Mule graphical mode**) icon in the activity bar to open the canvas UI if it doesnât open
    automatically.
3.  Add the connector the same way you added other components from the canvas UI:
    1.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    2.  In the **Add Component** panel, click **Connectors**.
    3.  Click the connector name and then click the operation to add, such as **Publish**:

        ![Add Publish operation from Anypoint MQ Connector](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-add-connector-operation.png)

        If the connector is not available locally, click the
        ![Search in Exchange icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-search-exchange.png)
        (**Search in Exchange**) toggle:

        ![Search in Exchange toggle](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-connector-search-exchange.png)

        <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Search locally</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Search in Exchange</td></tr></tbody></table>

    4.  Select the connector to add to your project.
    5.  Select the operation from the **Add Component** panel.

Adding a connector to an integration project does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Anypoint Code Builder workspace.

## Configure the Source For the Flow

A trigger (source) starts the flow when specified conditions are met. If a connector has its own
associated sources, you can use a connector-specific trigger to initiate the flow. Each connector
trigger has a unique configuration.

You can configure the following sources for this connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener trigger, follow these steps:

1.  Click the **Listener** component on the canvas UI.
2.  (Optional) Click the edit icon to change the name of the **Listener** component.
3.  (Optional) If you created a configuration file for the connector, select it.
4.  In the **General** tab, in the **Path** field, enter the relative path for the path set in the
    HTTP configuration element. See
    [Configure Paths](../../http-connector/latest/http-listener-ref#configure-paths).
5.  Select the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Allowed methods</p></td><td><p>To allow all HTTP methods (default), do not define this attribute. To restrict certain methods, specify all allowed HTTP methods, for example, <code>GET, POST</code>, as a comma-separated list.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response streaming mode</p></td><td><p>Whether to use streaming when the response is sent.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output MIME type</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameters</p></td><td><p>Appends a key-value pair for a reader property to the value of the Output MIME attribute. Multiple key-value pairs are allowed.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output encoding</p></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><p>Configures the way data streams are consumed in Mule apps.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary node only</p></td><td><p>Select this option to execute the <strong>Listener</strong> trigger on the primary node only when running in a cluster.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery policy</p></td><td><p>Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a redelivery policy to any source in a flow.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response</p></td><td><p>See <a href="../../http-connector/latest/http-documentation#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Response</p></td><td><p>HTTP error response returns a status code for the error.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><p>Body of the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><p>Headers to include in the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Status code</p></td><td><p>Status code of the error response, for example, <code>500</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason phrase</p></td><td><p>Reason for the error, for example, <code>Gateway Timeout</code> or <code>Internal Server Error</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add Components to Your Project

Add components to your integration project to build a flow:

1.  In the **Explorer** view, open the XML file for your project, such as `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Select **Build a Flow** from the start card to create an empty flow:

    ![Anypoint Code Builder canvas with starting cards with the options to start from scratch, build a flow, or build a subflow](../../connectors/_images/acb-build-flow.png)

3.  Change the name of the flow:
    1.  Click **Flow**.
    2.  Click the edit icon.

        ![Arrow pointing to the flow name edit pencil icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-change-flow-name.png)

    3.  Enter the flow name, and click the checkmark.
    4.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    5.  In the **Add Component** panel, search for and select your component from the results. The
        following example shows the **Listener** component from the **HTTP** search results:

        ![Listener component highlighted in the Add Component section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/main-tutorial-add-first-listener.png)

        The configuration XML file now includes the XML for the HTTP Listener into the `<flow/>`
        element, for example:

        ```XML
        <flow name="getFlights" >
          <http:listener path="" config-ref="" doc:name="Listener" doc:id="ojzfry" />
        </flow>
        ```

## Configure the Component Attributes

After you add components to your integration project, configure the attributes in the component UI,
or configure the attributes directly in the XML code editor.

Connector XML tags start with a namespace followed by an operation, following the syntax
`namespace:operation`, such as `<db:select/>` for a **Select** operation in Anypoint Connector for
Database (Database Connector).

You can use autocomplete from an expression (**fx**) field for a component to select DataWeave
functions from the Core module and other values accepted in this field.

## Create a New Configuration XML File

Anypoint Code Builder creates the initial Mule configuration XML file in `src/main/mule` within the
Mule project. This file contains global configurations and flow structures.

You can create additional configuration files, for example, to store global configurations.

To create a new XML configuration file:

1.  In the Explorer view, right-click the **mule** folder (`src/main/mule`) and select **New File**.

    Alternatively, use the menu.

    Show me how

    Select the `mule` folder, and then:
    - In the desktop IDE, select **File** > **New Mule Configuration File**.
    - In the cloud IDE, click the ![](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-menu.png) (menu)
      icon, and select **File** > **New File**.

2.  Enter a name for the new file with the `.xml` extension.
3.  In the Editor view for the new file, press Ctrl+Space to display available options.
4.  Select **Empty mule configuration**:

    ![New Mule configuration option in auto-correct](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-new-mule-config.png)

    This command adds the following code to the file:

    ```xml
    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <mule xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
        xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">

    </mule>
    ```

# Amazon Bedrock Connector 1.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

---

### Configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Config_Basic">Basic</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_Role">Role</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Basic

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Session Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The session token provided by Amazon STS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) when initially establishing a connection before the connector gives up and times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit used in the connection timeout configurations.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections.</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Socket Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) for data to be transferred over an established, open connection before the connection times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Socket Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit used in the socket timeout configurations.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The access key provided by Amazon.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Secret Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The secret key provided by Amazon.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the topic region endpoint.</p></td><td><p>us-east-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set this field to true to obtain credentials from the AWS environment. See <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">Using credentials providers in the AWS SDK for Java 2.x</a>.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Service Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets a custom service endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy host.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy port.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy username.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy password.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Domain</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy domain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workstation</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy workstation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Role

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Role ARN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The Role ARN uniquely identifies the role to assume to gain cross-account access.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom STS Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets a custom STS endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>External ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A unique identifier that might be required when you assume a role in another account. If the administrator of the account to which the role belongs provides an external ID, then provide that value in this field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) when initially establishing a connection before the connector gives up and times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit used in the connection timeout configurations.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections.</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Socket Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) for data to be transferred over an established, open connection before the connection times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Socket Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit used in the socket timeout configurations.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The access key provided by Amazon.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Secret Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The secret key provided by Amazon.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the topic region endpoint.</p></td><td><p>us-east-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set this field to true to obtain credentials from the AWS environment. See <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">Using credentials providers in the AWS SDK for Java 2.x</a>.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Service Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets a custom service endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy host.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy port.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy username.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy password.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Domain</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy domain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workstation</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The optional proxy workstation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [AGENT Chat](#AgentChat)
- [AGENT Chat Streaming (SSE)](#AgentChatStreamingSse)
- [AGENT Define Prompt Template](#AgentDefinePromptTemplate)
- [AGENT Get By Id](#AgentGetById)
- [AGENT List](#AgentList)
- [CHAT Answer Prompt](#ChatAnswerPrompt)
- [CHAT Answer Prompt Streaming](#ChatAnswerPromptStreaming)
- [EMBEDDING Adhoc Query](#EmbeddingAdhocQuery)
- [EMBEDDING Generate From Text](#EmbeddingGenerateFromText)
- [FOUNDATIONAL Model Details](#FoundationalModelDetails)
- [FOUNDATIONAL Models List](#FoundationalModelsList)
- [IMAGE Generate](#ImageGenerate)
- [SENTIMENT Analyze](#SentimentAnalyze)

## Operations

## AGENT Chat

`<ms-bedrock:agent-chat>`

Sends a chat message to a Bedrock agent and receives a response. The agent can use knowledge bases
and action groups to provide intelligent responses.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the agent to chat with.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Alias Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The alias ID of the agent version to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The userâs message or prompt to send to the agent.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enable Trace</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to enable trace logging for debugging agent reasoning.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Latency Optimized</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to optimize for lower latency at the cost of some functionality.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Session Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the session to maintain conversation context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Exclude Previous Thinking Steps</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to exclude previous thinking steps from the conversation history.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Previous Conversation Turns To Include</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of previous conversation turns to include for context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Knowledge Base Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the knowledge base to query.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Number Of Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of results to return from the query.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Override Search Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HYBRID</p></li><li><p>SEMANTIC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The search type to use for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retrieval Metadata Filter Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AND_ALL</p></li><li><p>OR_ALL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The filter type to apply to retrieval metadata.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Metadata Filters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>The metadata filters to apply to the retrieval query.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Knowledge Bases</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#KnowledgeBaseConfig">Knowledge Base Config</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The knowledge base configurations to use for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request timeout (overrides connector-level timeout if provided).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Unit of time for the request timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enable Retry</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enable retry for streaming operations on timeout failures.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Retries</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of retry attempts (0 = no retries).</p></td><td><p>3</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retry Backoff Ms</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base backoff delay in milliseconds for exponential backoff.</p></td><td><p>1000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request ID mapped from REQUEST-ID header to include in session-start event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Correlation Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Correlation ID mapped from CORRELATION-ID header to include in session-start event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID mapped from USER-ID header to include in session-start event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## AGENT Chat Streaming (SSE)

`<ms-bedrock:agent-chat-streaming-sse>`

Sends a chat message to a Bedrock agent and receives a streaming response using Server-Sent Events
(SSE). Returns responses in real-time as the agent generates them.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the agent to chat with.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Alias Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The alias ID of the agent version to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The userâs message or prompt to send to the agent.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enable Trace</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to enable trace logging for debugging agent reasoning.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Latency Optimized</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to optimize for lower latency at the cost of some functionality.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Session Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the session to maintain conversation context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Exclude Previous Thinking Steps</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to exclude previous thinking steps from the conversation history.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Previous Conversation Turns To Include</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of previous conversation turns to include for context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Knowledge Base Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the knowledge base to query.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Number Of Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of results to return from the query.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Override Search Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HYBRID</p></li><li><p>SEMANTIC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The search type to use for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retrieval Metadata Filter Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AND_ALL</p></li><li><p>OR_ALL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The filter type to apply to retrieval metadata.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Metadata Filters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>The metadata filters to apply to the retrieval query.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Knowledge Bases</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#KnowledgeBaseConfig">Knowledge Base Config</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The knowledge base configurations to use for retrieval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request timeout (overrides connector-level timeout if provided).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Unit of time for the request timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enable Retry</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enable retry for streaming operations on timeout failures.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Retries</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of retry attempts (0 = no retries).</p></td><td><p>3</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retry Backoff Ms</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base backoff delay in milliseconds for exponential backoff.</p></td><td><p>1000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request ID mapped from REQUEST-ID header to include in session-start event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Correlation Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Correlation ID mapped from CORRELATION-ID header to include in session-start event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID mapped from USER-ID header to include in session-start event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## AGENT Define Prompt Template

`<ms-bedrock:agent-define-prompt-template>`

Defines a prompt template with instructions and dataset for use in agent operations.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The prompt template string with placeholders for instructions and dataset.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Instructions</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Instructions to be inserted into the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Dataset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Dataset to be inserted into the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the foundation model to use.</p></td><td><p>amazon.nova-lite-v1:0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The AWS region where the model is deployed.</p></td><td><p>us-east-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A value between 0 and 1 that regulates the creativity of LLMs' responses. Use lower temperature if you want more deterministic responses, and use higher temperature if you want more creative or different responses for the same prompt from LLMs on Amazon Bedrock.</p></td><td><p>0.7</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The percentage of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token. It typically ranges between 0.9 and 0.95. Refer to the model provider documentation to confirm whether this parameter is supported and to verify the appropriate acceptable range.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top K</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token. It typically ranges between 0.4 and 0.6. Refer to the model provider documentation to confirm whether this parameter is supported and to verify the appropriate acceptable range.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Token Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of tokens to consume during output generation. For consistent and predictable responses, it is recommended to explicitly configure maxTokens based on the modelâs supported limits and expected output size.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Guardrail Identifier</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the guardrail to apply.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Guardrail Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The version of the guardrail to use.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Aws Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The AWS account ID for cross-account access.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## AGENT Get By Id

`<ms-bedrock:agent-get-by-id>`

Retrieves details of a Bedrock agent by its unique identifier.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the agent to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## AGENT List

`<ms-bedrock:agent-list>`

Lists all Bedrock agents available in the AWS account.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## CHAT Answer Prompt

`<ms-bedrock:chat-answer-prompt>`

Generates a text response from a prompt using the specified Bedrock foundation model.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The text prompt to send to the model.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the foundation model to use.</p></td><td><p>amazon.nova-lite-v1:0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The AWS region where the model is deployed.</p></td><td><p>us-east-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A value between 0 and 1 that regulates the creativity of LLMs' responses. Use lower temperature if you want more deterministic responses, and use higher temperature if you want more creative or different responses for the same prompt from LLMs on Amazon Bedrock.</p></td><td><p>0.7</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The percentage of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token. It typically ranges between 0.9 and 0.95. Refer to the model provider documentation to confirm whether this parameter is supported and to verify the appropriate acceptable range.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top K</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token. It typically ranges between 0.4 and 0.6. Refer to the model provider documentation to confirm whether this parameter is supported and to verify the appropriate acceptable range.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Token Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of tokens to consume during output generation. For consistent and predictable responses, it is recommended to explicitly configure maxTokens based on the modelâs supported limits and expected output size.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Guardrail Identifier</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the guardrail to apply.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Guardrail Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The version of the guardrail to use.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Aws Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The AWS account ID for cross-account access.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## CHAT Answer Prompt Streaming

`<ms-bedrock:chat-answer-prompt-streaming>`

Generates a streaming text response from a prompt using Server-Sent Events (SSE). Returns responses
in real-time as they are generated.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The text prompt to send to the model.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the foundation model to use.</p></td><td><p>amazon.nova-lite-v1:0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The AWS region where the model is deployed.</p></td><td><p>us-east-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A value between 0 and 1 that regulates the creativity of LLMs' responses. Use lower temperature if you want more deterministic responses, and use higher temperature if you want more creative or different responses for the same prompt from LLMs on Amazon Bedrock.</p></td><td><p>0.7</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The percentage of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token. It typically ranges between 0.9 and 0.95. Refer to the model provider documentation to confirm whether this parameter is supported and to verify the appropriate acceptable range.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top K</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of most-likely candidates that the model considers for the next token. It typically ranges between 0.4 and 0.6. Refer to the model provider documentation to confirm whether this parameter is supported and to verify the appropriate acceptable range.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Token Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of tokens to consume during output generation. For consistent and predictable responses, it is recommended to explicitly configure maxTokens based on the modelâs supported limits and expected output size.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Guardrail Identifier</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier of the guardrail to apply.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Guardrail Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The version of the guardrail to use.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Aws Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The AWS account ID for cross-account access.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## EMBEDDING Adhoc Query

`<ms-bedrock:embedding-adhoc-query>`

Performs Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) by converting the query and documents into embeddings,
comparing them for similarity, and ranking the most relevant document chunks to ground the response
with respect to the query.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The query text to search for in the document.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>File system path to the document file (PDF supported).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the embedding model to use.</p></td><td><p>amazon.titan-embed-text-v1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Dimension</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The dimensionality of the embedding vectors.</p></td><td><p>1024</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Normalize</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to normalize the embedding vectors.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Option Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of embedding output to generate.</p></td><td><p>FULL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## EMBEDDING Generate From Text

`<ms-bedrock:embedding-generate-from-text>`

Generates vector embeddings from text using Amazon Bedrock embedding models. Embeddings are
numerical representations of text that capture semantic meaning.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The text to generate embeddings for.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the embedding model to use.</p></td><td><p>amazon.titan-embed-text-v1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Dimension</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The dimensionality of the embedding vectors.</p></td><td><p>1024</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Normalize</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to normalize the embedding vectors.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## FOUNDATIONAL Model Details

`<ms-bedrock:foundational-model-details>`

Retrieves detailed information about a specific foundation model available in Amazon Bedrock.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the foundation model to use.</p></td><td><p>amazon.titan-text-express-v1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## FOUNDATIONAL Models List

`<ms-bedrock:foundational-models-list>`

Lists all foundation models available in Amazon Bedrock for the specified region.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## IMAGE Generate

`<ms-bedrock:image-generate>`

Generates an image from a text prompt using Amazon Bedrock image generation models (e.g., Stable
Diffusion, Amazon Titan Image).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Text To Image</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The text prompt describing the image to generate.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Avoid In Image</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Text describing what should be avoided in the generated image (negative prompt).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Full Path Output</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The full file path where the generated image is saved.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the image generation model to use.</p></td><td><p>amazon.titan-image-generator-v1:0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Num Of Images</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of images to generate.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Height</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The height of the generated image in pixels.</p></td><td><p>512</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Width</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The width of the generated image in pixels.</p></td><td><p>512</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cfg Scale</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values increase adherence to the prompt.</p></td><td><p>8.0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Seed</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The random seed for reproducible image generation.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-BEDROCK:ACCESS_DENIED
- MS-BEDROCK:AWS_SDK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:BEDROCK_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CLIENT_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-BEDROCK:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- MS-BEDROCK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-BEDROCK:SERVICE_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:THROTTLING_ERROR
- MS-BEDROCK:VALIDATION_ERROR

## SENTIMENT Analyze

`<ms-bedrock:sentiment-analyze>`

Analyzes the sentiment of the input text using Amazon Bedrock sentiment analysis models. Determines
whether the text expresses positive, negative, or neutral sentiment.

### Parameters
